title: conospermum huegelii
intro: Conospermum huegelii, commonly known as the yellow-john or lemon-scented conospermum, is a small, evergreen shrub native to Western Australia. The plant is known for its unique, lemon-scented leaves and has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties. The leaves and essential oil of Conospermum huegelii have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as skin irritations and respiratory problems. Conospermum huegelii can be prepared as a tea, essential oil, or tincture, and has been traditionally used to aid in digestion, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
